prose: This positive call rests on curated Parkinson's DirectEvidence [E1], which anchors the confidence tier and provides the sole basis for the mechanistic hypothesis. The evidence-ranked mechanistic story is coherent: the molecular initiating event is grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits, with the adverse outcome localized to the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2], consistent with an AOP pathway linking mitochondrial Complex-I disruption to selective neuronal loss. Human data strengthen plausibility, with case-series describing causal parkinsonism after exposure (Langston 1983) [E4], and structure-based prediction indicates brain penetrance, supporting CNS exposure feasibility [E5]. ToxCast coverage is thin, as expected for a research reagent [E3]; this assay sparseness is anti-diagnostic and offers no corroboration either way, so it neither supports nor undermines the call. The convergence of curated mechanism, cell-type grounding, and epidemiology makes this a well-supported mechanistic hypothesis, not a claim of established causation.

citations.detail: MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
